title: ClassificationRecipe
description:
A SEPAL Classification recipe. Trains a supervised classifier on labeled reference points and applies it pixel-by-pixel to one or more input images, producing a categorical land-cover map (and optionally per-class probabilities and a regression image).

**Pipeline.** (1) Stack input images plus optional auxiliary imagery (latitude, terrain, surface water) into a single multi-band feature image. (2) Sample feature values at each training point. (3) Train the classifier on (features → class) pairs. (4) Apply the trained classifier to every pixel in the AOI.

**Bands produced.** Always `class` (uint8 — pixel's predicted class label). For classifiers that support it (`RANDOM_FOREST`, `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST`, `CART`, `SVM`, `NAIVE_BAYES`): `class_probability` (probability of the predicted class, 0–100) and `probability_<value>` per class. For `RANDOM_FOREST`, `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST`, `CART` only: `regression` (continuous prediction, useful when class values are ordinal).

**AOI semantics.** Classification has no separate AOI field — the AOI is implicit in the input image(s) referenced from `inputImagery`. The classification covers whatever geometry those images cover.

This schema describes the persisted `model` object of an *initialized* recipe. Cross-field constraints that JSON Schema cannot express are enforced by the companion rules.

scale.description: Optional override for the per-pixel processing scale, in meters. When omitted, the recipe uses the input image's native scale (typically 30 m for Landsat-derived recipes, 10 m for Sentinel-2). Setting this changes the spatial unit at which training samples are extracted and predictions are generated. Higher values are coarser/cheaper; lower values approach native resolution.

inputImagery.title: ClassificationInputImagery
inputImagery.description: Source images that provide the per-pixel features the classifier learns on. At least one image is required. Each image is either a reference to another SEPAL recipe (whose output is used as input) or a direct Earth Engine asset id. Multiple images are allowed and are stacked band-wise; the classifier sees the union of their bands.

images.description: Array of input image specs. Order matters only insofar as duplicate band names from later images override earlier ones.

inputImage.title: ClassificationInputImage
inputImage.description: A single input image. The `type` discriminates between a SEPAL recipe reference and a raw Earth Engine asset; `id` is interpreted accordingly. `bandSetSpecs` controls which bands (and which derived bands) of this image are exposed to the classifier.

imageId.description: Stable id for this input image entry within the recipe (UUID). Used as the React list key and to address the image in subsequent panel actions; does NOT identify the underlying data source — that's `id`.

type.description:
What `id` points to.

- `RECIPE_REF` — `id` is the id of another SEPAL recipe (a mosaic, time-series, etc.). Its image output is used as input here.
- `ASSET` — `id` is an Earth Engine FeatureCollection / Image / ImageCollection asset id.

id.description: Either a SEPAL recipe id (when `type='RECIPE_REF'`) or an Earth Engine asset id (when `type='ASSET'`).

bands.description: All band names that the source image exposes. Populated by the GUI when the image is selected; the classifier's actual feature set is determined by `bandSetSpecs.included` plus derived bands. The LLM should only set this when constructing a recipe from scratch with known bands.

bandSetSpecs.description:
Specifications for which raw bands and which derived bands to expose to the classifier. Each spec is one of:

- `IMAGE_BANDS` — direct selection from the source image's bands. `included` is the list of band names to keep.
- `PAIR_WISE_EXPRESSION` — pairwise band combinations under one of the operations RATIO, NORMALIZED_DIFFERENCE, DIFFERENCE, DISTANCE (atan2/π), ANGLE (hypot). `included` is the list of source band names; the classifier sees one feature per pair.
- `INDEXES` — spectral indexes (NDVI, EVI, etc.). `included` is the list of index names to compute.

id.description: UUID of this spec, for stable React keys.

operation.description: Required only when `type='PAIR_WISE_EXPRESSION'`. The operation applied to each ordered pair of `included` bands.

included.description: Source band names (for IMAGE_BANDS / PAIR_WISE_EXPRESSION) or index names (for INDEXES) that this spec contributes to the classifier.

legend.title: ClassificationLegend
legend.description: The set of class labels the classifier is trained to predict. Every distinct integer in the training data must correspond to one entry; entries are sorted by `value` before training. The legend MUST contain at least one entry; in practice classification needs at least 2 classes to be meaningful.

id.description: UUID for stable React keys; not used by the classifier.

value.description: Integer class id stored in the output `class` band. Must be unique across entries.

label.description: Human-readable class name shown in the legend and visualizations.

color.description: Hex color (`#RRGGBB`) used for visualizations of this class.

trainingData.title: ClassificationTrainingData
trainingData.description:
Sources of labeled `(point, class)` reference data. The recipe samples each data set, joins them, and feeds the result to the classifier. At least one data set is required; multiple are unioned.

Each entry has a `type` discriminator. Most types require additional fields beyond `dataSetId`/`name`/`type` (csv/eeTable references, sampling parameters, column mappings, etc.) — the GUI populates these via a multi-step wizard. The LLM should default to a single `type='COLLECTED'` entry with empty `referenceData` (the user populates it interactively in the GUI).

trainingDataSet.title: ClassificationTrainingDataSet
trainingDataSet.description: A single source of reference points. The set of fields beyond the discriminator depends on `type`; this schema only constrains the shared shape and leaves the type-specific fields as `additionalProperties: true` because the GUI's wizard model is intricate and changes shape across versions.

dataSetId.description: UUID of this data set entry.

name.description: Display name shown in the panel.

type.description:
Source type.

- `COLLECTED` — points the user clicks on the map within the GUI's collect panel; stored in `referenceData`. The standard starting point for a fresh recipe.
- `RECIPE` — reference points come from another SEPAL recipe (typically another classification recipe used as a labeled ground-truth source). Requires a `recipe` id.
- `EE_TABLE` — reference points come from an Earth Engine FeatureCollection asset. Requires `eeTable`, location columns, and class column mapping.
- `CSV` — reference points come from an uploaded CSV. Requires column mappings.
- `SAMPLE_CLASSIFICATION` — sample points generated from an existing classification (asset or recipe). Requires `samplesPerClass` and `sampleScale`.
- `CEO` — Collect Earth Online project import. Requires institution/project ids.
- `CLASSIFICATION` — internal type used when the data set is itself a previous classification result; not normally constructed by the user.

referenceData.description: Collected reference points (used primarily by `type='COLLECTED'`). Each point has `x`/`y` in WGS84 decimal degrees and an integer `class`.

auxiliaryImagery.title: ClassificationAuxiliaryImagery
auxiliaryImagery.description:
Optional auxiliary feature layers added to every input pixel.

- `LATITUDE` — single band of pixel latitude in degrees. Useful when class boundaries vary with latitude (climate zones, biomes).
- `TERRAIN` — elevation, slope, aspect, eastness (sin(aspect)), northness (cos(aspect)) from SRTM 30 m. Strongly recommended in mountainous regions where terrain drives land-cover patterns.
- `WATER` — JRC Global Surface Water bands: occurrence, change, seasonality, max-extent, plus per-transition masks. Useful for water-related classifications.

Default `[]`. Each entry adds compute cost; pick only the layers relevant to the classification.

classifier.title: ClassificationClassifier
classifier.description:
The classifier algorithm and its hyperparameters. The `type` field discriminates among 7 algorithms; each has its own required parameters (enforced by the cross-field rules) and ignores the others. Reasonable defaults are provided for every parameter so that switching `type` does not require respecifying all fields.

Algorithm characteristics:
- `RANDOM_FOREST` (default) — robust, handles non-linear boundaries, low tuning effort. The default choice for most classifications. Supports class probability and regression outputs.
- `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST` — sequential boosting; can outperform Random Forest at the cost of more tuning. Supports the same outputs as RF.
- `CART` — single decision tree; fast and interpretable but prone to overfitting. Supports probability and regression.
- `NAIVE_BAYES` — assumes feature independence given class; cheap, often surprisingly competitive baselines. Supports probability but not regression.
- `SVM` — Support Vector Machine via libsvm. Many sub-modes (C-SVC, nu-SVC, one-class) and kernels (linear/poly/rbf/sigmoid). Sensitive to feature scaling — `normalize='YES'` is highly recommended.
- `MINIMUM_DISTANCE` — nearest-class-mean classifier. Trivially fast, generally a baseline rather than a final choice. Like SVM, benefits from normalization.
- `DECISION_TREE` — apply a pre-built decision tree (or ensemble) supplied as a string in the EE format. No training step.

numberOfTrees.description: Number of trees in the forest. Used by `RANDOM_FOREST` and `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST`. More trees → smoother decision surface, higher cost (linear in N). Default 25; values up to several hundred are common for production maps.

variablesPerSplit.description: Variables sampled per split (mtry). Used by `RANDOM_FOREST` only. `null` (default) lets EE pick √(numFeatures), the standard Random Forest heuristic. Setting it explicitly is unusual; lower values increase tree diversity at the cost of per-tree accuracy.

minLeafPopulation.description: Minimum number of training samples required to keep a leaf. Used by `RANDOM_FOREST` and `CART`. Higher values regularize (reduce overfitting) at the cost of bias. Default 1 — let the trees grow as deep as the data supports.

bagFraction.description: Fraction of training samples to bag for each tree. Used by `RANDOM_FOREST` only. Default 0.5. Lower fractions increase tree diversity (and out-of-bag generalization estimates); higher fractions use more data per tree.

maxNodes.description: Hard cap on nodes per tree. Used by `RANDOM_FOREST`, `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST`, `CART`. `null` (default) = unbounded — let the tree grow as deep as `minLeafPopulation` allows. Setting this is a regularization tool when trees overfit.

seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. Used by `RANDOM_FOREST` and `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST`. Default 1. Change to compare independent training runs; keep fixed for reproducible map outputs.

shrinkage.description: Learning rate for gradient boosting. Used by `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST` only. Default 0.005. Smaller values are more stable but require more trees; larger values converge faster but risk overshooting. Typical range 0.001–0.1.

samplingRate.description: Per-tree subsample rate for stochastic gradient boosting. Used by `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST` only. Default 0.7. Values < 1 introduce randomness across trees; 1.0 means deterministic full-data trees.

loss.description: Loss function for gradient boosting. Used by `GRADIENT_TREE_BOOST` only. `LeastAbsoluteDeviation` (default) is robust to outliers; `LeastSquares` is the textbook choice; `Huber` is in between.

lambda.description: Smoothing parameter for the categorical NB likelihood. Used by `NAIVE_BAYES` only. Default 0.000001 (essentially un-smoothed). Higher values regularize but bias towards the prior.

decisionProcedure.description: How libsvm aggregates per-class decisions in multiclass problems. Used by `SVM` only. `Voting` (default) is the standard pairwise vote; `Margin` uses the largest decision margin.

svmType.description:
libsvm SVM variant. Used by `SVM` only.

- `C_SVC` (default) — standard C-parameterized soft-margin classification.
- `NU_SVC` — ν-parameterized variant; ν bounds the fraction of margin errors and support vectors.
- `ONE_CLASS` — one-class novelty detection; valid only when the legend has exactly two classes (one positive, one negative); requires `oneClass` to identify the positive class.

kernelType.description: SVM kernel function. Used by `SVM` only. `LINEAR` (default) is the simplest and fastest; `RBF` is the typical non-linear choice; `POLY` and `SIGMOID` are specialized.

shrinking.description: Whether libsvm's shrinking heuristic is enabled. Used by `SVM` only. Default `true` — speeds up training without changing the result.

degree.description: Polynomial-kernel degree. Used by `SVM` with `kernelType='POLY'`. Default 3. Higher degrees fit more complex boundaries at the cost of training time and overfit risk.

gamma.description: Kernel coefficient for `POLY`, `RBF`, `SIGMOID`. Used by `SVM` only. `null` (default) lets EE pick `1/numFeatures` (the typical heuristic). Setting it explicitly is unusual; smaller γ means smoother decision surface.

coef0.description: Independent term in the kernel function for `POLY` and `SIGMOID`. Used by `SVM` only. Default 0. Modulates the influence of higher-degree terms in POLY and the SIGMOID activation midpoint.

cost.description: Soft-margin penalty C. Used by `SVM` with `svmType='C_SVC'`. Default 1. Higher values penalize misclassification more (less margin slack); lower values allow more slack.

nu.description: Margin/error fraction parameter ν. Used by `SVM` with `svmType='NU_SVC'`. Default 0.5. Acts as an upper bound on the fraction of margin errors and a lower bound on the fraction of support vectors.

oneClass.description: Class value identifying the positive class for one-class classification. Used by `SVM` with `svmType='ONE_CLASS'`. Must match one of the two legend entries' `value`. Required only in this mode.

metric.description: Distance metric for `MINIMUM_DISTANCE`. Default `euclidean`. `cosine` is angle-only; `mahalanobis` accounts for class-internal covariance; `manhattan` (L1) is robust to outliers. Earth Engine implements `euclidean`, `cosine`, and `mahalanobis`; `manhattan` is exposed for forward compatibility but may not be supported on all backends.

decisionTree.description: A decision tree in the EE serialized format, OR a JSON-array string of multiple trees (treated as an ensemble). Used by `DECISION_TREE` only — no training is performed; the tree is applied directly. Typically pasted from another classifier's `.explain()` output or from a hand-authored ruleset.

tileScale.description: Tile-scale multiplier passed to EE reducers used by the classifier. Used by all training-based classifiers (everything except `DECISION_TREE`); particularly relevant for `SVM` and `MINIMUM_DISTANCE` where normalization runs a `reduceRegion`. Default 1. Increase (2, 4, 8) to avoid out-of-memory errors on large AOIs at the cost of more compute time.

normalize.description: Whether to per-band unit-scale the input image before training. Used by `SVM` and `MINIMUM_DISTANCE` only — both are sensitive to feature scale. Default `YES`. `NO` skips normalization (useful only if the inputs are already on a comparable scale).

allOf.description: SVM and MINIMUM_DISTANCE require tileScale and normalize.
